system_prompt:
You are a senior website architecture planner and conversion-focused information architect.

Your job is to decide the optimal page structure for a small business website based on business context, audience, offer, and conversion goal.

You are not trying to maximize page count.
You are trying to create the smallest high-performing website that:
- clearly explains the business
- builds trust fast
- supports the primary CTA
- reduces buyer hesitation
- matches the real needs of this business type

How to think:
- Think like a strategist, not a sitemap generator.
- Every page must earn its existence.
- If a goal can be handled well by a homepage section, do not create a separate page.
- Create separate pages only when they improve clarity, trust, SEO discoverability, or conversion flow.
- Favor simple, conversion-focused site structures for small businesses.
- The homepage should do the heaviest lifting and be the most comprehensive page.
- Use the business type, offer complexity, service area, and trust requirements to decide page count.

Decision rules:
- Always include a homepage with slug "home" and isHomepage: true.
- Include a contact page, unless the site is intentionally kept very small and the homepage already contains a strong contact section and CTA.
- Maximum 8 pages.
- Each page must have a unique lowercase hyphenated slug.
- Suggested sections must only use this list:
  hero, trust-bar, features, benefits, problem-solution, how-it-works, services, testimonials, results, pricing, faq, cta-band, contact, footer, gallery, service-area, about-team
- Every page must end with footer.
- Do not create redundant pages with slightly different wording.
- Do not create unsupported page categories.
- If trust is a major purchase driver, prioritize about, testimonials, results, faq, and service clarity.
- If location matters, include service-area where it helps conversion.
- If pricing clarity matters, include pricing.
- If the offer is unfamiliar or process-heavy, include how-it-works and/or problem-solution.
- If the service is visual, consider gallery.

Return JSON with this exact structure:
{
  "siteGoal": "string",
  "targetAudience": "string",
  "pages": [
    {
      "slug": "home",
      "title": "Page Title",
      "purpose": "What this page achieves",
      "pageType": "local-business|service-business|saas|coach|product-sales",
      "isHomepage": true,
      "suggestedSections": ["hero", "features", "testimonials", "cta-band", "footer"]
    }
  ]
}

[Playbook 2 | business=Veterinary doctors / independent vets | section=BUYER_BEHAVIOR | type=Independent veterinary practice]

Business: Veterinary doctors / independent vets
Group: Pet and animal care
Business Type: Independent veterinary practice
Section: BUYER_BEHAVIOR

**Buying Journey Type:** Moderate friction with high trust considerations  

**User Intent Pattern:**  
Buyers typically want to know:  
- Is this veterinary clinic suitable for my pet's needs?  
- What are the qualifications of the veterinary team?  
- How do I book an appointment?  
- Is there availability for urgent care?  
- What are the costs associated with services?  

**What Increases Conversion:**  
- Clear service descriptions and pricing  
- Strong trust indicators (e.g., certifications, testimonials)  
- Easy online booking process  
- Clear communication of emergency services  
- FAQ section addressing common concerns  

**What Increases Bounce:**  
- Vague service descriptions  
- Lack of visible trust indicators  
- Complicated booking process  
- No clear emergency care information  
- Overly decorative design that distracts from key information

**User Intent Pattern:**  
The buyer typically wants to understand:  
- Are you the right fit for my pet's issues?  
- What is your approach to behavior modification?  
- How do I book a consultation?  
- What can I expect in terms of results?  

**What Increases Conversion:**  
- Clear, reassuring messaging about expertise  
- Easy navigation to book consultations  
- Testimonials and success stories prominently displayed  
- Clear descriptions of services offered  
- Strong visuals of happy pets and owners  

**What Increases Bounce:**  
- Vague service descriptions  
- Complicated booking process  
- Lack of trust signals or testimonials  
- No clear explanation of the consultation process  
- Overly technical language or jargon

system_prompt:
You are a brand system designer and visual direction strategist for small business websites.

Create cohesive site-wide settings for a small business website.

Rules:
- Return JSON only
- themeVariant must be one of: clean, bold, premium, playful
- designMode must be one of: soft-glass, editorial-luxe, warm-service, clinical-structured
- primaryColor, secondaryColor, accentColor must be valid hex colors
- footer links must use href
- keep actions lean and practical
- avoid random trendy choices that do not fit the business

Design mode guidance:
- soft-glass = rounded, layered, translucent, modern SaaS-like
- editorial-luxe = sharper typography, restrained surfaces, premium editorial feel
- warm-service = approachable, practical, warmer surfaces, clear utility
- clinical-structured = orderly, calm, minimal decoration, trust-first

system_prompt:
Design the homepage direction for this business before content planning begins.

Your task:
Define the homepage design direction in a way that will materially improve downstream section planning and rendering.

Focus on:
- above-the-fold strategy
- hero composition
- headline support structure
- CTA prominence
- early trust placement
- navigation behavior
- typography hierarchy
- color role strategy
- section pacing
- density vs whitespace balance
- scanability
- visual rhythm from top to bottom
- how the design should reduce hesitation and improve conversion

Important:
- Be specific and directive.
- Do not describe vague aesthetics.
- Do not just say “clean modern layout” or “use whitespace.”
- Make decisions that a renderer or frontend system could actually use.
- Tie your design direction to the business, audience, offer, and CTA.
- If the business is service-based, make sure the design direction supports trust, clarity, and action.
- If the audience is busy or comparison-shopping, prioritize quick comprehension and friction reduction.
- If objections are likely, reflect how design should handle them visually and structurally.

heroStrategy.layout: Centered layout with a large, engaging image of a successful visa applicant or family, accompanied by a clear service offering statement.
heroStrategy.goal: To immediately convey the value of VisaBridge Consultants and encourage users to seek help with their visa applications.
heroStrategy.headlineStyle: Bold and action-oriented, focusing on the primary benefit, e.g., 'Your Trusted Partner for Visa Success'.
heroStrategy.supportingCopyStyle: Concise and reassuring, emphasizing the urgency and expertise of services, e.g., 'Fast, reliable visa assistance tailored to your needs.'
heroStrategy.ctaApproach: Make the primary CTA obvious and low-friction
heroStrategy.trustSupport: Include a small testimonial or success rate badge below the headline to establish credibility right away.

navigationStrategy.style: Simple and intuitive, with a sticky navigation bar that remains visible as users scroll.
navigationStrategy.ctaPlacement: The primary CTA should be included in the navigation bar for easy access at any time.
navigationStrategy.notes: Navigation should support quick comprehension and be easy to use, minimizing any friction in finding information.

visualSystem.designIntent: Professional and approachable, using a clean layout that emphasizes clarity and trust.
visualSystem.primaryColorRole: Deep blue should be used for headings and key elements to convey reliability.
visualSystem.secondaryColorRole: Light gray should serve as a background for sections to create a clean and organized look.
visualSystem.accentColorRole: Bright colors (like a vibrant green or orange) should be used for CTA buttons to ensure they stand out.
visualSystem.contrastStrategy: Use strong contrast between text and background colors to enhance readability and focus on key areas.
visualSystem.surfaceStyle: Cards and backgrounds should feel light and airy, with subtle shadows to create depth without overwhelming the user.

typographyStrategy.headingApproach: Headings should be bold and modern, using a sans-serif font for clarity.
typographyStrategy.bodyApproach: Body text should be clean and straightforward, using a legible serif or sans-serif font.
typographyStrategy.hierarchyNotes: Use varying font sizes and weights to create a clear hierarchy that guides scanning and comprehension.
typographyStrategy.fontPairingDirection: Pair a bold sans-serif font for headings with a clean, readable serif or sans-serif font for body text.

sectionFlowStrategy.openingExperience: The hero section should capture attention and convey the main offer, followed by a snapshot of services to quickly inform users.
sectionFlowStrategy.midPageRhythm: Alternate between benefits of services and testimonials to create a rhythm that builds trust and interest.
sectionFlowStrategy.closingExperience: End with a strong CTA band that reinforces the offer and encourages immediate action.
sectionFlowStrategy.densityPattern: Balance dense sections (like testimonials) with lighter sections (like benefits) to maintain engagement without overwhelming users.

conversionStrategy.primaryFrictionToReduce: Concerns about cost and skepticism about the effectiveness of consultancy services.
conversionStrategy.howDesignSupportsTrust: Incorporate testimonials, success rates, and professional imagery to visually support trust.
conversionStrategy.howDesignSupportsAction: Ensure CTAs are visually prominent and repeated throughout the page, making it easy for users to take action.
whatMustNotHappen: Users should not feel lost or confused about what to do next.
Important information should not be buried in dense text.

implementationNotes: Ensure mobile responsiveness is prioritized, with touch-friendly buttons and easy navigation.
Use high-quality images that resonate with the target audience, showcasing diverse clients and successful outcomes.

system_prompt:
Review this homepage design brief strictly before page planning starts.

Your job:
Decide whether this design brief is genuinely strong enough to guide a high-converting homepage.

Review it for:
- clarity of above-the-fold direction
- hero strength
- CTA support
- trust placement
- navigation usefulness
- typography usefulness
- color role clarity
- visual rhythm
- section pacing
- bounce-risk
- specificity
- practical usefulness for downstream planning and rendering

Strict evaluation rules:
- Fail it if it is generic, vague, or too aesthetic without enough conversion logic.
- Fail it if it sounds good but would still produce a bland homepage.
- Fail it if hero direction is weak or underdeveloped.
- Fail it if color or typography advice is not role-based.
- Fail it if section pacing is shallow or disconnected from the buyer journey.
- Fail it if it does not materially help the next system make better homepage decisions.

When reviewing, ask:
- Would this brief produce a homepage that feels clearer and more effective than a generic template?
- Does it actually reduce buyer hesitation?
- Does it give enough concrete direction for hierarchy, emphasis, and action?
- Is it tied to this business, audience, and offer?
- Is it specific enough to guide a renderer or page planner?

system_prompt:
Design the homepage direction for this business before content planning begins.

Your task:
Define the homepage design direction in a way that will materially improve downstream section planning and rendering.

Focus on:
- above-the-fold strategy
- hero composition
- headline support structure
- CTA prominence
- early trust placement
- navigation behavior
- typography hierarchy
- color role strategy
- section pacing
- density vs whitespace balance
- scanability
- visual rhythm from top to bottom
- how the design should reduce hesitation and improve conversion

Important:
- Be specific and directive.
- Do not describe vague aesthetics.
- Do not just say “clean modern layout” or “use whitespace.”
- Make decisions that a renderer or frontend system could actually use.
- Tie your design direction to the business, audience, offer, and CTA.
- If the business is service-based, make sure the design direction supports trust, clarity, and action.
- If the audience is busy or comparison-shopping, prioritize quick comprehension and friction reduction.
- If objections are likely, reflect how design should handle them visually and structurally.

heroStrategy.layout: Full-width background image of a successful visa applicant or family in an emotional moment (e.g., celebrating a visa approval), overlaying a concise headline and a prominent CTA button.
heroStrategy.goal: To evoke an emotional response while clearly communicating VisaBridge's value proposition and prompting users to take immediate action.
heroStrategy.headlineStyle: Bold, impactful, and benefit-driven, e.g., 'Navigate Your Visa Journey with Confidence and Expertise'.
heroStrategy.supportingCopyStyle: Short, reassuring statements that highlight urgency and expertise, e.g., 'Fast, reliable visa assistance tailored to your unique needs.'
heroStrategy.ctaApproach: Make the primary CTA obvious and low-friction
heroStrategy.trustSupport: Display a small badge or quote from a client testimonial beneath the CTA, along with a success rate percentage (e.g., '98% Success Rate in Visa Approvals') to establish immediate credibility.

navigationStrategy.style: Sticky navigation bar that remains at the top as users scroll, with clear, descriptive links.
navigationStrategy.ctaPlacement: Include the primary CTA ('Get Your Free Consultation') in the navigation bar, ensuring it stands out with a bright accent color.
navigationStrategy.notes: Navigation should be straightforward and user-friendly, allowing quick access to key information and minimizing any friction in the user journey.

visualSystem.designIntent: Professional yet approachable, with a clean layout that emphasizes clarity and trustworthiness.
visualSystem.primaryColorRole: Deep blue should be used for headings and key elements to convey reliability and professionalism.
visualSystem.secondaryColorRole: Light gray should serve as a background color to create a clean and organized look, enhancing readability.
visualSystem.accentColorRole: Bright colors (like vibrant green or orange) should be used for CTA buttons and important highlights to ensure they stand out and attract attention.
visualSystem.contrastStrategy: Utilize strong contrast between text and background colors to enhance readability, especially for key information and CTAs, while using softer tones for less critical areas.
visualSystem.surfaceStyle: Cards and backgrounds should feel light and airy, incorporating subtle shadows to create depth and separate sections without overwhelming the user.

typographyStrategy.headingApproach: Headings should be bold, modern, and action-oriented, using a sans-serif font for clarity and impact.
typographyStrategy.bodyApproach: Body text should be clean, straightforward, and legible, using a sans-serif font for easy reading.
typographyStrategy.hierarchyNotes: Use varying font sizes and weights to create a clear hierarchy that guides scanning, ensuring that key points stand out.
typographyStrategy.fontPairingDirection: Pair a bold sans-serif font for headings with a clean, readable sans-serif font for body text to maintain a cohesive and professional appearance.

sectionFlowStrategy.openingExperience: The hero section should capture attention and convey the main offer, immediately followed by a snapshot of services that briefly outlines VisaBridge's offerings.
sectionFlowStrategy.midPageRhythm: Alternate between sections that explain the benefits of services and showcase testimonials, creating a rhythm that builds trust and interest while addressing potential objections.
sectionFlowStrategy.closingExperience: Conclude with a strong CTA band that reinforces the offer and encourages immediate action, supported by a final testimonial or success story to alleviate skepticism.
sectionFlowStrategy.densityPattern: Balance dense sections (like testimonials and benefits) with lighter sections (like service snapshots) to maintain engagement and prevent overwhelming users with information.

conversionStrategy.primaryFrictionToReduce: Concerns about the cost of consultancy services and skepticism regarding effectiveness.
conversionStrategy.howDesignSupportsTrust: Visually support trust by incorporating testimonials, success rates, and professional imagery throughout the homepage, especially in the hero and testimonials sections.
conversionStrategy.howDesignSupportsAction: Ensure CTAs are visually prominent and strategically placed throughout the page, making it easy for users to take action at any point in their journey.
whatMustNotHappen: Users should not feel lost or confused about what to do next; clear CTAs must guide them.
Important information should not be buried in dense text; key points should be highlighted and easily scannable.

implementationNotes: Ensure the design is mobile-responsive, with touch-friendly buttons and intuitive navigation for a seamless experience across devices.
Use high-quality, relatable images that resonate with the target audience, showcasing diverse clients and successful outcomes to enhance emotional connection.

_review.summary: The design brief lacks specificity and depth in several key areas, which could lead to a bland and ineffective homepage.

issues.message: The hero strategy is underdeveloped; it lacks a compelling emotional hook and clear prioritization of content.
The conversion strategy does not adequately address how to overcome skepticism about costs and effectiveness.
The brief lacks concrete examples and specific guidance for typography and color roles.
The section flow strategy does not provide a clear connection to the buyer journey, risking disconnection in pacing.

requiredFixes: Enhance the hero section with a stronger emotional appeal and clearer content hierarchy.
Provide more specific guidance on typography and color roles based on their intended functions.
Clarify the connection between sections to ensure they align with the buyer journey and maintain user engagement.
strengths: The brief identifies the target audience and business context clearly.

Business Playbook Guidance:
Retrieved business playbook guidance for query: service-business | Comprehensive visa application support and urgent case handling. | site architecture homepage sections section order objections trust conversion

[Playbook 1 | business=Veterinary doctors / independent vets | section=SITE_ARCHITECTURE_OPTION_A | type=Independent veterinary practice]

Business: Veterinary doctors / independent vets
Group: Pet and animal care
Business Type: Independent veterinary practice
Section: SITE_ARCHITECTURE_OPTION_A

**ARCHITECTURE_TYPE:** SINGLE_PAGE_SITE  
**When this is the right choice:**  
- The clinic is early-stage or looking for a quick online presence  
- Services are straightforward and easily explained  
- The main conversion goal is appointment bookings  
- Speed of launch is a priority  

### PAGE_STRUCTURE  
**Page Count:** 1  
**Page:** Home  

**Suggested Section Order:**  
- Hero  
- Trust bar  
- Services overview  
- Benefits  
- How it works  
- Testimonials  
- Contact / Booking CTA  
- FAQ  
- Footer  

**WHY THIS ARCHITECTURE WORKS**  
This architecture effectively addresses the buyer's intent with a concise flow that quickly answers high-priority questions regarding services, trust, and booking.

**RISKS OF SINGLE_PAGE**  
- Limited SEO opportunities  
- Less room for detailed service explanations  
- Can feel shallow if the business expands  

**BEST USE CASE**  
- Fast launch for a new clinic  
- Simple service offerings with a focus on immediate bookings

[Playbook 2 | business=Occupational therapy centers | section=SITE_ARCHITECTURE_OPTION_B | type=Healthcare / Therapy]

Business: Occupational therapy centers
Group: Healthcare, wellness, therapy, and rehabilitation
Business Type: Healthcare / Therapy
Section: SITE_ARCHITECTURE_OPTION_B

**ARCHITECTURE_TYPE:** MULTI_PAGE_SITE  
**When this is the right choice:**  
- The business plans to grow and offer diverse services  
- SEO and content depth are priorities  
- The brand aims for a more established online presence  

**PAGE_STRUCTURE**  
**Page 01:** Home  
**Purpose:** Introduce the business, establish value, and drive bookings.  
**Sections:**  
- Hero  
- Trust bar  
- Services overview  
- Benefits  
- Testimonials  
- CTA band  
- Footer  

**Page 02:** Services  
**Purpose:** Detail individual therapy services and benefits.  
**Sections:**  
- Hero  
- Service descriptions  
- Benefits  
- Pricing  
- FAQ  
- CTA band  
- Footer  

**Page 03:** About  
**Purpose:** Build trust through team bios and clinic philosophy.  
**Sections:**  
- Hero  
- Team introductions  
- Clinic mission  
- Testimonials  
- Footer  

**Page 04:** Contact  
**Purpose:** Provide clear booking paths and contact options.  
**Sections:**  
- Hero  
- Contact form  
- FAQ  
- Footer  

**WHY THIS ARCHITECTURE WORKS**  
This structure allows for detailed exploration of services, building trust through transparency and expertise, and facilitating conversions through clear contact pathways.

**RISKS OF MULTI_PAGE**  
- More content creation required  
- Potentially overwhelming for users if not designed well  

**BEST USE CASE**  
- Established practices looking for growth  
- SEO-focused businesses

[Playbook 3 | business=Elder care / assisted home care services | section=SITE_ARCHITECTURE_OPTION_B | type=Healthcare / Assisted Home Care]

Business: Elder care / assisted home care services
Group: Healthcare, wellness, therapy, and rehabilitation
Business Type: Healthcare / Assisted Home Care
Section: SITE_ARCHITECTURE_OPTION_B

### ARCHITECTURE_TYPE: MULTI_PAGE_SITE

#### When this is the right choice:
- The business wants stronger SEO  
- Multiple service categories exist that need explanation  
- The brand aims to feel more established  
- Trust and discoverability need to improve over time

### PAGE_STRUCTURE

**Page 01:** Home  
**Purpose:** Introduce the business, establish the value proposition, create immediate trust, and drive booking.  
**Sections:**
- Hero  
- Trust bar  
- Services overview  
- Benefits  
- Testimonials  
- CTA band  
- Footer  

**Page 02:** Services  
**Purpose:** Explain each service in detail (companionship, personal care, etc.).  
**Sections:**
- Hero  
- Service details  
- Benefits  
- Pricing  
- FAQ  
- CTA band  
- Footer  

**Page 03:** About  
**Purpose:** Build emotional trust around the caregivers, care philosophy, and company mission.  
**Sections:**
- Hero  
- Problem-solution  
- About team  
- Testimonials  
- Footer  

**Page 04:** Contact / Book  
**Purpose:** Provide the clearest booking path possible.  
**Sections:**
- Hero  
- Contact form  
- FAQ  
- Footer

### WHY_THIS_ARCHITECTURE_WORKS
This structure separates major decision layers:
- Home = overall persuasion  
- Services = detailed offer clarity  
- About = emotional trust  
- Contact = clear action

### RISKS_OF_MULTI_PAGE
- More content required  
- More design and development effort  
- If done poorly, can feel too big for a small business  
- Weak pages may reduce quality instead of improving it

### BEST_USE_CASE
- Serious local growth  
- SEO-minded business owners  
- Businesses wanting more established positioning  
- Operators with time to maintain more content

Archetype: saas

Planner rules:
- Do not default to `hero -> features/benefits -> testimonials -> cta-band -> footer` unless the business genuinely calls for it.
- Match the section mix to the business archetype, buying journey, and trust needs.
- Visual-first businesses should usually show proof of the output or atmosphere early.
- Local/mobile services should usually explain the process or service-area earlier than generic marketing sites.
- Trust-sensitive services should usually introduce problem/solution, results, or practitioner credibility before the close.
- Use distinct variants intentionally. If you pick `cards`, `image-cards`, `story`, `single-highlight`, `dual`, or `legal-heavy`, assume the renderer will make them look different.

Recommended starting direction:

  },

system_prompt:
You are an elite homepage designer, conversion copywriter, and structured UI content architect.

Role:
- You are proactive.
- You make strong design decisions based on the business context.
- Produce one complete homepage PageDocument.

Rules:
- Use the exact planned section types and variants
- Do not use placeholder copy
- Avoid generic filler such as "we are the best" or "your trusted partner"
- Mention the actual service, audience, and location when possible
- Use schema-safe content keys
- The page should feel complete, premium, and intentionally sequenced
- Add layoutHint and motionHint where useful
- Use richer section content when appropriate: eyebrow, supportingPoints, trustChips, badge, ctaNote

- Likely Objections: Concerns about cost, Skepticism about the effectiveness of consultancy

- Fonts: Arial, sans-serif / Verdana, sans-serif
- Theme Variant: clean

timeline.title: Personalized Consultation
timeline.description: Kickstart your visa journey with a complimentary consultation. Our experienced consultants will evaluate your unique circumstances, clarify visa requirements, and outline a tailored application strategy designed to optimize your chances of approval.

timeline.title: Thorough Document Preparation
timeline.description: Receive a customized document checklist to ensure you gather all necessary paperwork. Our meticulous team reviews your documents for accuracy and completeness, helping you avoid common pitfalls that could delay your application.

timeline.title: Timely Application Submission
timeline.description: With our expert guidance, submit your application accurately and punctually. We ensure that every form is completed correctly and that all supporting documents are included, significantly enhancing your likelihood of success.

timeline.title: Comprehensive Interview Coaching
timeline.description: Prepare for your visa interview with confidence through our specialized coaching. We provide tailored mock interviews and practical tips to help you navigate the process effectively, ensuring you present your best self to visa officials.

timeline.title: Celebrate Your Approval
timeline.description: Once your visa is approved, we continue to support you with essential information and resources for a smooth transition to your new destination, ensuring you feel ready and informed for your next chapter.

system_prompt:
You are a senior website content writer and section composer.

Your job is to write one homepage section with substantial, specific, usable content.

You must use:
- the business context
- the design brief
- the page plan
- the section keyword targets

Requirements:
- Return JSON only for one complete section object
- Keep the same section type and generally keep the same variant
- Make the content specific to the business, audience, offer, and location when relevant
- Write enough content so the section does not feel thin or token
- Use the provided keyword targets naturally, without stuffing
- Make the section commercially useful, readable, and visually fillable
- Respect the section's role in the page sequence
- For service/trust/proof sections, reduce hesitation and improve clarity

Minimum substance:
- hero: meaningful headline, subheading, CTA, supporting points, trust support
- features/services/benefits: enough real items to feel like a legitimate section
- testimonials: believable quotes with useful signal
- faq: real questions that reduce hesitation
- footer: complete and navigable
user_prompt:
Write one complete homepage section.

Business Context:
- Business Name: VisaBridge Consultants
- Business Type: service-business
- Description: A visa and immigration consultancy based in Bangalore, specializing in document review, application filing, interview preparation, and urgent case guidance for students, professionals, and families.
- Target Audience: Individuals and families seeking efficient and clear visa assistance.
- Primary CTA: Get Your Free Consultation
- Main Offer: Comprehensive visa application support and urgent case handling.
- Location: Bangalore
- Differentiators: Expertise in various visa categories, Fast response times for urgent cases
- Trust Drivers: Client testimonials, Success rates in visa approvals
- Likely Objections: Concerns about cost, Skepticism about the effectiveness of consultancy

Design Brief:
{
  "heroStrategy": {
    "layout": "Full-width background image of a successful visa applicant or family in an emotional moment (e.g., celebrating a visa approval), overlaying a concise headline and a prominent CTA button.",
    "goal": "To evoke an emotional response while clearly communicating VisaBridge's value proposition and prompting users to take immediate action.",
    "headlineStyle": "Bold, impactful, and benefit-driven, e.g., 'Navigate Your Visa Journey with Confidence and Expertise'.",
    "supportingCopyStyle": "Short, reassuring statements that highlight urgency and expertise, e.g., 'Fast, reliable visa assistance tailored to your unique needs.'",
    "ctaApproach": "Make the primary CTA obvious and low-friction",
    "trustSupport": "Display a small badge or quote from a client testimonial beneath the CTA, along with a success rate percentage (e.g., '98% Success Rate in Visa Approvals') to establish immediate credibility."
  },
  "navigationStrategy": {
    "style": "Sticky navigation bar that remains at the top as users scroll, with clear, descriptive links.",
    "links": [
      "Home",
      "Services",
      "Testimonials",
      "FAQ",
      "Contact"
    ],
    "ctaPlacement": "Include the primary CTA ('Get Your Free Consultation') in the navigation bar, ensuring it stands out with a bright accent color.",
    "notes": "Navigation should be straightforward and user-friendly, allowing quick access to key information and minimizing any friction in the user journey."
  },
  "visualSystem": {
    "designIntent": "Professional yet approachable, with a clean layout that emphasizes clarity and trustworthiness.",
    "primaryColorRole": "Deep blue should be used for headings and key elements to convey reliability and professionalism.",
    "secondaryColorRole": "Light gray should serve as a background color to create a clean and organized look, enhancing readability.",
    "accentColorRole": "Bright colors (like vibrant green or orange) should be used for CTA buttons and important highlights to ensure they stand out and attract attention.",
    "contrastStrategy": "Utilize strong contrast between text and background colors to enhance readability, especially for key information and CTAs, while using softer tones for less critical areas.",
    "surfaceStyle": "Cards and backgrounds should feel light and airy, incorporating subtle shadows to create depth and separate sections without overwhelming the user."
  },
  "typographyStrategy": {
    "headingApproach": "Headings should be bold, modern, and action-oriented, using a sans-serif font for clarity and impact.",
    "bodyApproach": "Body text should be clean, straightforward, and legible, using a sans-serif font for easy reading.",
    "hierarchyNotes": "Use varying font sizes and weights to create a clear hierarchy that guides scanning, ensuring that key points stand out.",
    "fontPairingDirection": "Pair a bold sans-serif font for headings with a clean, readable sans-serif font for body text to maintain a cohesive and professional appearance."
  },
  "sectionFlowStrategy": {
    "openingExperience": "The hero section should capture attention and convey the main offer, immediately followed by a snapshot of services that briefly outlines VisaBridge's offerings.",
    "midPageRhythm": "Alternate between sections that explain the benefits of services and showcase testimonials, creating a rhythm that builds trust and interest while addressing potential objections.",
    "closingExperience": "Conclude with a strong CTA band that reinforces the offer and encourages immediate action, supported by a final testimonial or success story to alleviate skepticism.",
    "densityPattern": "Balance dense sections (like testimonials and benefits) with lighter sections (like service snapshots) to maintain engagement and prevent overwhelming users with information."
  },
  "conversionStrategy": {
    "primaryFrictionToReduce": "Concerns about the cost of consultancy services and skepticism regarding effectiveness.",
    "howDesignSupportsTrust": "Visually support trust by incorporating testimonials, success rates, and professional imagery throughout the homepage, especially in the hero and testimonials sections.",
    "howDesignSupportsAction": "Ensure CTAs are visually prominent and strategically placed throughout the page, making it easy for users to take action at any point in their journey.",
    "whatMustNotHappen": [
      "Users should not feel lost or confused about what to do next; clear CTAs must guide them.",
      "Important information should not be buried in dense text; key points should be highlighted and easily scannable."
    ]
  },
  "implementationNotes": [
    "Ensure the design is mobile-responsive, with touch-friendly buttons and intuitive navigation for a seamless experience across devices.",
    "Use high-quality, relatable images that resonate with the target audience, showcasing diverse clients and successful outcomes to enhance emotional connection."
  ],
  "_meta": {
    "phase": "design_rewrite",
    "attempt": 1
  },

[Guide 1 | section=hero | variant=centered | type=guide]

### Example 1: Minimalist Hero Section

**When To Use**: Use this design for a clean and modern landing page that focuses on a single product or service.

**Why It Works**: The use of ample white space and a bold heading creates a strong focal point, while the contrasting colors enhance readability and draw attention to the CTA.

**Tailwind Notes**:
- Utilizes flexbox for vertical centering.
- Responsive typography ensures readability across devices.
- Strong contrast between background and text for accessibility.

```html
<section class='flex items-center justify-center h-screen bg-gray-900 text-white text-center px-4'>
  <div>
    <h1 class='text-5xl font-bold mb-4'>Transform Your Business</h1>
    <p class='text-xl mb-8'>Unlock the potential of your company with our innovative solutions.</p>
    <a href='#' class='bg-blue-600 hover:bg-blue-700 text-white font-semibold py-3 px-6 rounded-lg transition duration-300'>Get Started</a>
  </div>
</section>
```

### Example 2: Bold Imagery Hero Section

**When To Use**: Ideal for brands that want to showcase their products with impactful visuals while maintaining a strong message.

**Why It Works**: The background image adds depth and context, while the overlay ensures text remains legible. The CTA button is prominently displayed to encourage user interaction.

**Tailwind Notes**:
- Background image with overlay for readability.
- Large text sizes for emphasis on key messages.
- Button styling encourages clicks with hover effects.

```html
<section class='relative flex items-center justify-center h-screen bg-cover bg-center' style='background-image: url(https://example.com/hero-image.jpg)'>
  <div class='absolute inset-0 bg-black opacity-50'></div>
  <div class='relative text-white text-center px-4'>
    <h1 class='text-6xl font-extrabold mb-4'>Your Journey Begins Here</h1>
    <p class='text-lg mb-8'>Join us to explore the world of possibilities.</p>
    <a href='#' class='bg-red-600 hover:bg-red-700 text-white font-semibold py-3 px-6 rounded-lg transition duration-300'>Learn More</a>
  </div>
</section>
```

### Example 3: Split Hero Section with Call to Action

**When To Use**: Best for services that require a brief explanation alongside a strong call to action, appealing to both visual and textual learners.

**Why It Works**: The split layout allows for a visual representation of the service while clearly communicating the value proposition. The CTA is distinct and easy to find.

**Tailwind Notes**:
- Flexbox layout provides a responsive design.
- Clear separation of text and imagery aids comprehension.
- Consistent padding and margins create a balanced layout.

```html
<section class='flex flex-col md:flex-row items-center justify-between h-screen bg-white px-8'>
  <div class='md:w-1/2 mb-8 md:mb-0'>

  </div>
</section>
```

system_prompt:
You are a senior website content writer and section composer.

Your job is to write one homepage section with substantial, specific, usable content.

You must use:
- the business context
- the design brief
- the page plan
- the section keyword targets

Requirements:
- Return JSON only for one complete section object
- Keep the same section type and generally keep the same variant
- Make the content specific to the business, audience, offer, and location when relevant
- Write enough content so the section does not feel thin or token
- Use the provided keyword targets naturally, without stuffing
- Make the section commercially useful, readable, and visually fillable
- Respect the section's role in the page sequence
- For service/trust/proof sections, reduce hesitation and improve clarity

Minimum substance:
- hero: meaningful headline, subheading, CTA, supporting points, trust support
- features/services/benefits: enough real items to feel like a legitimate section
- testimonials: believable quotes with useful signal
- faq: real questions that reduce hesitation
- footer: complete and navigable
user_prompt:
Write one complete homepage section.

Business Context:
- Business Name: VisaBridge Consultants
- Business Type: service-business
- Description: A visa and immigration consultancy based in Bangalore, specializing in document review, application filing, interview preparation, and urgent case guidance for students, professionals, and families.
- Target Audience: Individuals and families seeking efficient and clear visa assistance.
- Primary CTA: Get Your Free Consultation
- Main Offer: Comprehensive visa application support and urgent case handling.
- Location: Bangalore
- Differentiators: Expertise in various visa categories, Fast response times for urgent cases
- Trust Drivers: Client testimonials, Success rates in visa approvals
- Likely Objections: Concerns about cost, Skepticism about the effectiveness of consultancy

Design Brief:
{
  "heroStrategy": {
    "layout": "Full-width background image of a successful visa applicant or family in an emotional moment (e.g., celebrating a visa approval), overlaying a concise headline and a prominent CTA button.",
    "goal": "To evoke an emotional response while clearly communicating VisaBridge's value proposition and prompting users to take immediate action.",
    "headlineStyle": "Bold, impactful, and benefit-driven, e.g., 'Navigate Your Visa Journey with Confidence and Expertise'.",
    "supportingCopyStyle": "Short, reassuring statements that highlight urgency and expertise, e.g., 'Fast, reliable visa assistance tailored to your unique needs.'",
    "ctaApproach": "Make the primary CTA obvious and low-friction",
    "trustSupport": "Display a small badge or quote from a client testimonial beneath the CTA, along with a success rate percentage (e.g., '98% Success Rate in Visa Approvals') to establish immediate credibility."
  },
  "navigationStrategy": {
    "style": "Sticky navigation bar that remains at the top as users scroll, with clear, descriptive links.",
    "links": [
      "Home",
      "Services",
      "Testimonials",
      "FAQ",
      "Contact"
    ],
    "ctaPlacement": "Include the primary CTA ('Get Your Free Consultation') in the navigation bar, ensuring it stands out with a bright accent color.",
    "notes": "Navigation should be straightforward and user-friendly, allowing quick access to key information and minimizing any friction in the user journey."
  },
  "visualSystem": {
    "designIntent": "Professional yet approachable, with a clean layout that emphasizes clarity and trustworthiness.",
    "primaryColorRole": "Deep blue should be used for headings and key elements to convey reliability and professionalism.",
    "secondaryColorRole": "Light gray should serve as a background color to create a clean and organized look, enhancing readability.",
    "accentColorRole": "Bright colors (like vibrant green or orange) should be used for CTA buttons and important highlights to ensure they stand out and attract attention.",
    "contrastStrategy": "Utilize strong contrast between text and background colors to enhance readability, especially for key information and CTAs, while using softer tones for less critical areas.",
    "surfaceStyle": "Cards and backgrounds should feel light and airy, incorporating subtle shadows to create depth and separate sections without overwhelming the user."
  },
  "typographyStrategy": {
    "headingApproach": "Headings should be bold, modern, and action-oriented, using a sans-serif font for clarity and impact.",
    "bodyApproach": "Body text should be clean, straightforward, and legible, using a sans-serif font for easy reading.",
    "hierarchyNotes": "Use varying font sizes and weights to create a clear hierarchy that guides scanning, ensuring that key points stand out.",
    "fontPairingDirection": "Pair a bold sans-serif font for headings with a clean, readable sans-serif font for body text to maintain a cohesive and professional appearance."
  },
  "sectionFlowStrategy": {
    "openingExperience": "The hero section should capture attention and convey the main offer, immediately followed by a snapshot of services that briefly outlines VisaBridge's offerings.",
    "midPageRhythm": "Alternate between sections that explain the benefits of services and showcase testimonials, creating a rhythm that builds trust and interest while addressing potential objections.",
    "closingExperience": "Conclude with a strong CTA band that reinforces the offer and encourages immediate action, supported by a final testimonial or success story to alleviate skepticism.",
    "densityPattern": "Balance dense sections (like testimonials and benefits) with lighter sections (like service snapshots) to maintain engagement and prevent overwhelming users with information."
  },

[Guide 1 | section=trust-bar | variant=simple | type=guide]

### Example 1: Trust Badges with Descriptive Text

**When To Use**: Use this layout to build credibility on a landing page by showcasing trust badges alongside brief descriptions.

**Why It Works**: The combination of icons and text creates a clear and visually appealing hierarchy, making it easy for users to quickly understand the value propositions. The use of contrasting colors for the badges ensures they stand out.

**Tailwind Notes**:
- Flexbox is used for alignment and spacing, ensuring responsiveness.
- Consistent padding and margin create a clean layout.
- Text colors and sizes are chosen for readability and emphasis.

```html
<section class='bg-white p-6 md:p-10 shadow-lg rounded-lg'><div class='flex flex-wrap justify-center space-x-8'><div class='flex flex-col items-center'><img src='trust-badge1.png' alt='Trust Badge 1' class='w-16 h-16 mb-2'><p class='text-gray-700 text-center'>Secure Payments</p></div><div class='flex flex-col items-center'><img src='trust-badge2.png' alt='Trust Badge 2' class='w-16 h-16 mb-2'><p class='text-gray-700 text-center'>30-Day Guarantee</p></div><div class='flex flex-col items-center'><img src='trust-badge3.png' alt='Trust Badge 3' class='w-16 h-16 mb-2'><p class='text-gray-700 text-center'>Customer Support</p></div></div></section>
```

### Example 2: Customer Testimonials with Trust Indicators

**When To Use**: This layout is ideal for displaying customer testimonials alongside trust indicators to build confidence in your product or service.

**Why It Works**: The testimonials are presented in a card format, which gives each one its own space, enhancing readability. The use of shadows and rounded corners adds a polished feel, while the trust indicators reinforce credibility.

**Tailwind Notes**:
- Grid layout ensures responsiveness across devices.
- Subtle shadows and rounded corners provide a modern aesthetic.
- The use of typography emphasizes the testimonials and trust indicators.

```html
<section class='bg-gray-50 p-8'><div class='max-w-6xl mx-auto grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-6'><div class='bg-white p-5 rounded-lg shadow-md'><p class='text-lg font-semibold text-gray-800'>“Amazing service! Highly recommend.”</p><p class='text-gray-600 mt-2'>- Jane Doe</p><div class='flex justify-center mt-4'><img src='trust-badge1.png' alt='Trust Badge' class='w-10 h-10'><img src='trust-badge2.png' alt='Trust Badge' class='w-10 h-10 ml-2'></div></div><div class='bg-white p-5 rounded-lg shadow-md'><p class='text-lg font-semibold text-gray-800'>“Best purchase I've made this year!”</p><p class='text-gray-600 mt-2'>- John Smith</p><div class='flex justify-center mt-4'><img src='trust-badge1.png' alt='Trust Badge' class='w-10 h-10'><img src='trust-badge2.png' alt='Trust Badge' class='w-10 h-10 ml-2'></div></div><div class='bg-white p-5 rounded-lg shadow-md'><p class='text-lg font-semibold text-gray-800'>“Fantastic quality and support!”</p><p class='text-gray-600 mt-2'>- Alex Johnson</p><div class='flex justify-center mt-4'><img src='trust-badge1.png' alt='Trust Badge' class='w-10 h-10'><img src='trust-badge2.png' alt='Trust Badge' class='w-10 h-10 ml-2'></div></div></div></section>
```

[Guide 3 | section=trust-bar | variant=with-icons | type=guide]

### Example 1: Brand Trust Badges

**When To Use**: When showcasing partnerships or certifications to build credibility.

**Why It Works**: The use of icons alongside text creates a visual hierarchy that draws attention to trusted brands, enhancing perceived reliability.

**Tailwind Notes**:
- Utilizes flexbox for alignment and spacing.
- Incorporates responsive design for mobile compatibility.
- Emphasizes contrast with background and text colors.

```html
<section class="bg-white py-8">
  <div class="container mx-auto text-center">
    <h2 class="text-2xl font-bold mb-6">Trusted by Leading Brands</h2>
    <div class="flex flex-wrap justify-center space-x-6">
      <div class="flex items-center p-4 border border-gray-200 rounded-lg shadow-md">
        <img src="/path/to/icon1.svg" alt="Brand 1" class="h-12 w-12 mr-3">
        <span class="text-lg font-medium">Brand 1</span>
      </div>
      <div class="flex items-center p-4 border border-gray-200 rounded-lg shadow-md">
        <img src="/path/to/icon2.svg" alt="Brand 2" class="h-12 w-12 mr-3">
        <span class="text-lg font-medium">Brand 2</span>
      </div>
      <div class="flex items-center p-4 border border-gray-200 rounded-lg shadow-md">
        <img src="/path/to/icon3.svg" alt="Brand 3" class="h-12 w-12 mr-3">
        <span class="text-lg font-medium">Brand 3</span>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>
```

### Example 2: Security and Compliance Icons

**When To Use**: Ideal for emphasizing security features or compliance certifications to instill confidence in users.

**Why It Works**: The combination of icons and concise text communicates trust quickly, while the layout ensures that each element is easily digestible.

**Tailwind Notes**:
- Uses grid layout for a clean, organized presentation.
- Incorporates ample padding for touch targets on mobile devices.
- Contrast is enhanced through background and icon colors.

```html
<section class="bg-gray-50 py-10">
  <div class="container mx-auto text-center">

Business: Elder care / assisted home care services
Group: Healthcare, wellness, therapy, and rehabilitation
Business Type: Healthcare / Assisted Home Care
Section: HOMEPAGE_IDEATION_OPTION_2

### DESIGN_DIRECTION: DIRECT_CARE_CONVERSION

#### DESIGN_GOAL
- Clear and straightforward  
- Fast action-oriented  
- Commercially focused  
- Trustworthy and professional

#### VISUAL_CHARACTER
**Mood:** Direct, organized, efficient  
**Emotional Tone:** Professional and supportive  
**Density:** Moderate, with a focus on actionable information  
**Scan Pattern:** Strong top-down conversion flow

#### COLOR_DIRECTION
**Primary Color Role:** Deep blue for trust and reliability  
**Secondary Color Role:** Light gray for clarity  
**Accent Color Role:** Bright color for CTAs

#### TYPOGRAPHY_DIRECTION
**Heading Style:** Bold and clear  
**Body Style:** Clean and straightforward  
**Hierarchy Goal:** Fast comprehension and low bounce rates

#### HERO_DIRECTION
**Hero Layout:** Centered with a strong CTA cluster  
**Hero Content Priorities:**
- What services are offered  
- Why in-home care is beneficial  
- Visible booking button

#### MID_PAGE_FLOW
- Hero  
- Snapshot of services  
- Benefits of in-home care  
- Testimonials  
- FAQ  
- Final CTA

### WHY_THIS DESIGN_WORKS
This design prioritizes fast booking and clear communication, which are essential for families needing immediate care solutions.

### BEST_FOR
- Lead generation focused elder care services  
- Businesses prioritizing quick decision-making  
- Practical service providers

system_prompt:
You are a senior website content writer and section composer.

Your job is to write one homepage section with substantial, specific, usable content.

You must use:
- the business context
- the design brief
- the page plan
- the section keyword targets

Requirements:
- Return JSON only for one complete section object
- Keep the same section type and generally keep the same variant
- Make the content specific to the business, audience, offer, and location when relevant
- Write enough content so the section does not feel thin or token
- Use the provided keyword targets naturally, without stuffing
- Make the section commercially useful, readable, and visually fillable
- Respect the section's role in the page sequence
- For service/trust/proof sections, reduce hesitation and improve clarity

Minimum substance:
- hero: meaningful headline, subheading, CTA, supporting points, trust support
- features/services/benefits: enough real items to feel like a legitimate section
- testimonials: believable quotes with useful signal
- faq: real questions that reduce hesitation
- footer: complete and navigable
user_prompt:
Write one complete homepage section.

Business Context:
- Business Name: VisaBridge Consultants
- Business Type: service-business
- Description: A visa and immigration consultancy based in Bangalore, specializing in document review, application filing, interview preparation, and urgent case guidance for students, professionals, and families.
- Target Audience: Individuals and families seeking efficient and clear visa assistance.
- Primary CTA: Get Your Free Consultation
- Main Offer: Comprehensive visa application support and urgent case handling.
- Location: Bangalore
- Differentiators: Expertise in various visa categories, Fast response times for urgent cases
- Trust Drivers: Client testimonials, Success rates in visa approvals
- Likely Objections: Concerns about cost, Skepticism about the effectiveness of consultancy

Design Brief:
{
  "heroStrategy": {
    "layout": "Full-width background image of a successful visa applicant or family in an emotional moment (e.g., celebrating a visa approval), overlaying a concise headline and a prominent CTA button.",
    "goal": "To evoke an emotional response while clearly communicating VisaBridge's value proposition and prompting users to take immediate action.",
    "headlineStyle": "Bold, impactful, and benefit-driven, e.g., 'Navigate Your Visa Journey with Confidence and Expertise'.",
    "supportingCopyStyle": "Short, reassuring statements that highlight urgency and expertise, e.g., 'Fast, reliable visa assistance tailored to your unique needs.'",
    "ctaApproach": "Make the primary CTA obvious and low-friction",
    "trustSupport": "Display a small badge or quote from a client testimonial beneath the CTA, along with a success rate percentage (e.g., '98% Success Rate in Visa Approvals') to establish immediate credibility."
  },
  "navigationStrategy": {
    "style": "Sticky navigation bar that remains at the top as users scroll, with clear, descriptive links.",
    "links": [
      "Home",
      "Services",
      "Testimonials",
      "FAQ",
      "Contact"
    ],
    "ctaPlacement": "Include the primary CTA ('Get Your Free Consultation') in the navigation bar, ensuring it stands out with a bright accent color.",
    "notes": "Navigation should be straightforward and user-friendly, allowing quick access to key information and minimizing any friction in the user journey."
  },
  "visualSystem": {
    "designIntent": "Professional yet approachable, with a clean layout that emphasizes clarity and trustworthiness.",
    "primaryColorRole": "Deep blue should be used for headings and key elements to convey reliability and professionalism.",
    "secondaryColorRole": "Light gray should serve as a background color to create a clean and organized look, enhancing readability.",
    "accentColorRole": "Bright colors (like vibrant green or orange) should be used for CTA buttons and important highlights to ensure they stand out and attract attention.",
    "contrastStrategy": "Utilize strong contrast between text and background colors to enhance readability, especially for key information and CTAs, while using softer tones for less critical areas.",
    "surfaceStyle": "Cards and backgrounds should feel light and airy, incorporating subtle shadows to create depth and separate sections without overwhelming the user."
  },
  "typographyStrategy": {
    "headingApproach": "Headings should be bold, modern, and action-oriented, using a sans-serif font for clarity and impact.",
    "bodyApproach": "Body text should be clean, straightforward, and legible, using a sans-serif font for easy reading.",
    "hierarchyNotes": "Use varying font sizes and weights to create a clear hierarchy that guides scanning, ensuring that key points stand out.",
    "fontPairingDirection": "Pair a bold sans-serif font for headings with a clean, readable sans-serif font for body text to maintain a cohesive and professional appearance."
  },
  "sectionFlowStrategy": {
    "openingExperience": "The hero section should capture attention and convey the main offer, immediately followed by a snapshot of services that briefly outlines VisaBridge's offerings.",
    "midPageRhythm": "Alternate between sections that explain the benefits of services and showcase testimonials, creating a rhythm that builds trust and interest while addressing potential objections.",
    "closingExperience": "Conclude with a strong CTA band that reinforces the offer and encourages immediate action, supported by a final testimonial or success story to alleviate skepticism.",
    "densityPattern": "Balance dense sections (like testimonials and benefits) with lighter sections (like service snapshots) to maintain engagement and prevent overwhelming users with information."
  },
  "conversionStrategy": {
    "primaryFrictionToReduce": "Concerns about the cost of consultancy services and skepticism regarding effectiveness.",
    "howDesignSupportsTrust": "Visually support trust by incorporating testimonials, success rates, and professional imagery throughout the homepage, especially in the hero and testimonials sections.",
    "howDesignSupportsAction": "Ensure CTAs are visually prominent and strategically placed throughout the page, making it easy for users to take action at any point in their journey.",
    "whatMustNotHappen": [
      "Users should not feel lost or confused about what to do next; clear CTAs must guide them.",
      "Important information should not be buried in dense text; key points should be highlighted and easily scannable."
    ]
  },
  "implementationNotes": [

    "Use high-quality, relatable images that resonate with the target audience, showcasing diverse clients and successful outcomes to enhance emotional connection."

Variant Implementation Guidance:
Retrieved Tailwind guidance for trust-bar / simple:

Use the exact selected variant first. You may borrow composition ideas from alternative variants of the same section family only if they strengthen the chosen design direction without changing the section type.

[Guide 1 | section=trust-bar | variant=simple | type=guide]

### Example 1: Trust Badges with Descriptive Text

**When To Use**: Use this layout to build credibility on a landing page by showcasing trust badges alongside brief descriptions.

**Why It Works**: The combination of icons and text creates a clear and visually appealing hierarchy, making it easy for users to quickly understand the value propositions. The use of contrasting colors for the badges ensures they stand out.

**Tailwind Notes**:
- Flexbox is used for alignment and spacing, ensuring responsiveness.
- Consistent padding and margin create a clean layout.
- Text colors and sizes are chosen for readability and emphasis.

```html
<section class='bg-white p-6 md:p-10 shadow-lg rounded-lg'><div class='flex flex-wrap justify-center space-x-8'><div class='flex flex-col items-center'><img src='trust-badge1.png' alt='Trust Badge 1' class='w-16 h-16 mb-2'><p class='text-gray-700 text-center'>Secure Payments</p></div><div class='flex flex-col items-center'><img src='trust-badge2.png' alt='Trust Badge 2' class='w-16 h-16 mb-2'><p class='text-gray-700 text-center'>30-Day Guarantee</p></div><div class='flex flex-col items-center'><img src='trust-badge3.png' alt='Trust Badge 3' class='w-16 h-16 mb-2'><p class='text-gray-700 text-center'>Customer Support</p></div></div></section>
```

### Example 2: Customer Testimonials with Trust Indicators

**When To Use**: This layout is ideal for displaying customer testimonials alongside trust indicators to build confidence in your product or service.

**Why It Works**: The testimonials are presented in a card format, which gives each one its own space, enhancing readability. The use of shadows and rounded corners adds a polished feel, while the trust indicators reinforce credibility.

**Tailwind Notes**:
- Grid layout ensures responsiveness across devices.
- Subtle shadows and rounded corners provide a modern aesthetic.
- The use of typography emphasizes the testimonials and trust indicators.

```html
<section class='bg-gray-50 p-8'><div class='max-w-6xl mx-auto grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-6'><div class='bg-white p-5 rounded-lg shadow-md'><p class='text-lg font-semibold text-gray-800'>“Amazing service! [Guide 3 | section=trust-bar | variant=with-icons | type=guide]

### Example 1: Brand Trust Badges

**When To Use**: When showcasing partnerships or certifications to build credibility.

**Why It Works**: The use of icons alongside text creates a visual hierarchy that draws attention to trusted brands, enhancing perceived reliability.

**Tailwind Notes**:
- Utilizes flexbox for alignment and spacing.
- Incorporates responsive design for mobile compatibility.
- Emphasizes contrast with background and text colors.

```html
<section class="bg-white py-8">
  <div class="container mx-auto text-center">
    <h2 class="text-2xl font-bold mb-6">Trusted by Leading Brands</h2>
    <div class="flex flex-wrap justify-center space-x-6">
      <div class="flex items-center p-4 border border-gray-200 rounded-lg shadow-md">
        <img src="/path/to/icon1.svg" alt="Brand 1" class="h-12 w-12 mr-3">
        <span class="text-lg font-medium">Brand 1</span>
      </div>
      <div class="flex items-center p-4 border border-gray-200 rounded-lg shadow-md">
        <img src="/path/to/icon2.svg" alt="Brand 2" class="h-12 w-12 mr-3">
        <span class="text-lg font-medium">Brand 2</span>
      </div>
      <div class="flex items-center p-4 border border-gray-200 rounded-lg shadow-md">
        <img src="/path/to/icon3.svg" alt="Brand 3" class="h-12 w-12 mr-3">
        <span class="text-lg font-medium">Brand 3</span>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>
```

### Example 2: Security and Compliance Icons

**When To Use**: Ideal for emphasizing security features or compliance certifications to instill confidence in users.

**Why It Works**: The combination of icons and concise text communicates trust quickly, while the layout ensures that each element is easily digestible.

**Tailwind Notes**:
- Uses grid layout for a clean, organized presentation.
- Incorporates ample padding for touch targets on mobile devices.
- Contrast is enhanced through background and icon colors.

```html
<section class="bg-gray-50 py-10">
  <div class="container mx-auto text-center">
    <h2 class="text-3xl font-bold mb-5">Your Security is Our Priority</h2>
    <div class="grid grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-4 gap-6">
      <div class="flex flex-col items-center p-6 bg-white rounded-lg shadow-lg">
        <img src="/path/to/security-icon.svg" alt="Secure" class="h-16 w-16 mb-4">
        <span class="text-lg font-semibold">Secure Payments</span>
      </div>
      <div class="flex flex-col items-center p-6 bg-white rounded-lg shadow-lg">
        <img src="/path/to/compliance-icon.svg" alt="Compliance" class="h-16 w-16 mb-4">
        <span class="text-lg font-semibold">GDPR Compliant</span>
      </div>
      <div class="flex flex-col items-center p-6 bg-white rounded-lg shadow-lg">
        <img src="/path/to/encryption-icon.svg" alt="Encryption" class="h-16 w-16 mb-4">
        <span class="text-lg font-semibold">End-to-End Encryption</span>
      </div>
      <div class="flex flex-col items-center p-6 bg-white rounded-lg shadow-lg">
        <img src="/path/to/verified-icon.svg" alt="Verified" class="h-16 w-16 mb-4">
        <span class="text-lg font-semibold">Verified by Experts</span>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</section>
```

Variant Implementation Guidance:
Retrieved Tailwind guidance for features / icon-grid:

Use the exact selected variant first. You may borrow composition ideas from alternative variants of the same section family only if they strengthen the chosen design direction without changing the section type.

[Guide 1 | section=features | variant=icon-grid | type=guide]

### Example 1: Feature Highlights with Icons

**When To Use**: Use this layout to showcase key features of a product or service in a visually appealing grid format.

**Why It Works**: The grid layout allows for easy scanning of features, while the use of icons adds a visual element that enhances understanding and retention. The intentional spacing and typography create a clean, professional look.

**Tailwind Notes**:
- Flexbox is used for responsive layout adjustments.
- Icons provide visual cues that improve comprehension.
- Consistent padding and margin create a balanced layout.

```html
<section class='py-16 bg-gray-50'><div class='container mx-auto'><h2 class='text-3xl font-bold text-center mb-8'>Our Key Features</h2><div class='grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-8'><div class='bg-white p-6 rounded-lg shadow-md text-center'><div class='mb-4'><svg class='w-12 h-12 mx-auto text-blue-500' fill='currentColor' viewBox='0 0 20 20'><path d='M10 2a8 8 0 100 16 8 8 0 000-16z'/></svg></div><h3 class='text-xl font-semibold mb-2'>Feature One</h3><p class='text-gray-600'>Description of feature one that highlights its benefits and usage.</p></div><div class='bg-white p-6 rounded-lg shadow-md text-center'><div class='mb-4'><svg class='w-12 h-12 mx-auto text-blue-500' fill='currentColor' viewBox='0 0 20 20'><path d='M10 2a8 8 0 100 16 8 8 0 000-16z'/></svg></div><h3 class='text-xl font-semibold mb-2'>Feature Two</h3><p class='text-gray-600'>Description of feature two that highlights its benefits and usage.</p></div><div class='bg-white p-6 rounded-lg shadow-md text-center'><div class='mb-4'><svg class='w-12 h-12 mx-auto text-blue-500' fill='currentColor' viewBox='0 0 20 20'><path d='M10 2a8 8 0 100 16 8 8 0 000-16z'/></svg></div><h3 class='text-xl font-semibold mb-2'>Feature Three</h3><p class='text-gray-600'>Description of feature three that highlights its benefits and usage.</p></div></div></div></section>
```

### Example 2: Feature Icons with Call to Action

**When To Use**: This layout is ideal for emphasizing features while driving users to take action, such as signing up or learning more.

**Why It Works**: The combination of feature icons and a strong CTA encourages user engagement. The layout is responsive and maintains visual hierarchy, ensuring that the most important information stands out.

**Tailwind Notes**:
- Use of flexbox for alignment of the CTA with the feature grid.
- Color contrast between background and text enhances readability.
- Hover effects on buttons improve interactivity.

```html
<section class='py-16 bg-white'><div class='container mx-auto'><h2 class='text-3xl font-bold text-center mb-8'>Discover Our Features</h2><div class='grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-10'><div class='bg-gray-100 p-6 rounded-lg shadow-lg text-center'><div class='mb-4'><svg class='w-12 h-12 mx-auto text-green-500' fill='currentColor' viewBox='0 0 20 20'><path d='M10 2a8 8 0 100 16 8 8 0 000-16z'/></svg></div><h3 class='text-xl font-semibold mb-2'>Feature A</h3><p class='text-gray-700'>Brief description that explains the feature and its impact.</p></div><div class='bg-gray-100 p-6 rounded-lg shadow-lg text-center'><div class='mb-4'><svg class='w-12 h-12 mx-auto text-green-500' fill='currentColor' viewBox='0 0 20 20'><path d='M10 2a8 8 0 100 16 8 8 0 000-16z'/></svg></div><h3 class='text-xl font-semibold mb-2'>Feature B</h3><p class='text-gray-700'>Brief description that explains the feature and its impact.</p></di

[Guide 2 | section=features | variant=registry | type=registry]

system_prompt:
You are a senior website content writer and section composer.

Your job is to write one homepage section with substantial, specific, usable content.

You must use:
- the business context
- the design brief
- the page plan
- the section keyword targets

Requirements:
- Return JSON only for one complete section object
- Keep the same section type and generally keep the same variant
- Make the content specific to the business, audience, offer, and location when relevant
- Write enough content so the section does not feel thin or token
- Use the provided keyword targets naturally, without stuffing
- Make the section commercially useful, readable, and visually fillable
- Respect the section's role in the page sequence
- For service/trust/proof sections, reduce hesitation and improve clarity

Minimum substance:
- hero: meaningful headline, subheading, CTA, supporting points, trust support
- features/services/benefits: enough real items to feel like a legitimate section
- testimonials: believable quotes with useful signal
- faq: real questions that reduce hesitation
- footer: complete and navigable

user_prompt:
Write one complete homepage section.

Business Context:
- Business Name: VisaBridge Consultants
- Business Type: service-business
- Description: A visa and immigration consultancy based in Bangalore, specializing in document review, application filing, interview preparation, and urgent case guidance for students, professionals, and families.
- Target Audience: Individuals and families seeking efficient and clear visa assistance.
- Primary CTA: Get Your Free Consultation
- Main Offer: Comprehensive visa application support and urgent case handling.
- Location: Bangalore
- Differentiators: Expertise in various visa categories, Fast response times for urgent cases
- Trust Drivers: Client testimonials, Success rates in visa approvals
- Likely Objections: Concerns about cost, Skepticism about the effectiveness of consultancy

Design Brief:
{
  "heroStrategy": {
    "layout": "Full-width background image of a successful visa applicant or family in an emotional moment (e.g., celebrating a visa approval), overlaying a concise headline and a prominent CTA button.",
    "goal": "To evoke an emotional response while clearly communicating VisaBridge's value proposition and prompting users to take immediate action.",
    "headlineStyle": "Bold, impactful, and benefit-driven, e.g., 'Navigate Your Visa Journey with Confidence and Expertise'.",
    "supportingCopyStyle": "Short, reassuring statements that highlight urgency and expertise, e.g., 'Fast, reliable visa assistance tailored to your unique needs.'",
    "ctaApproach": "Make the primary CTA obvious and low-friction",
    "trustSupport": "Display a small badge or quote from a client testimonial beneath the CTA, along with a success rate percentage (e.g., '98% Success Rate in Visa Approvals') to establish immediate credibility."
  },
  "navigationStrategy": {
    "style": "Sticky navigation bar that remains at the top as users scroll, with clear, descriptive links.",
    "links": [
      "Home",
      "Services",
      "Testimonials",
      "FAQ",
      "Contact"
    ],
    "ctaPlacement": "Include the primary CTA ('Get Your Free Consultation') in the navigation bar, ensuring it stands out with a bright accent color.",
    "notes": "Navigation should be straightforward and user-friendly, allowing quick access to key information and minimizing any friction in the user journey."
  },
  "visualSystem": {
    "designIntent": "Professional yet approachable, with a clean layout that emphasizes clarity and trustworthiness.",
    "primaryColorRole": "Deep blue should be used for headings and key elements to convey reliability and professionalism.",
    "secondaryColorRole": "Light gray should serve as a background color to create a clean and organized look, enhancing readability.",
    "accentColorRole": "Bright colors (like vibrant green or orange) should be used for CTA buttons and important highlights to ensure they stand out and attract attention.",
    "contrastStrategy": "Utilize strong contrast between text and background colors to enhance readability, especially for key information and CTAs, while using softer tones for less critical areas.",
    "surfaceStyle": "Cards and backgrounds should feel light and airy, incorporating subtle shadows to create depth and separate sections without overwhelming the user."
  },
  "typographyStrategy": {
    "headingApproach": "Headings should be bold, modern, and action-oriented, using a sans-serif font for clarity and impact.",
    "bodyApproach": "Body text should be clean, straightforward, and legible, using a sans-serif font for easy reading.",
    "hierarchyNotes": "Use varying font sizes and weights to create a clear hierarchy that guides scanning, ensuring that key points stand out.",
    "fontPairingDirection": "Pair a bold sans-serif font for headings with a clean, readable sans-serif font for body text to maintain a cohesive and professional appearance."
  },
  "sectionFlowStrategy": {
    "openingExperience": "The hero section should capture attention and convey the main offer, immediately followed by a snapshot of services that briefly outlines VisaBridge's offerings.",
    "midPageRhythm": "Alternate between sections that explain the benefits of services and showcase testimonials, creating a rhythm that builds trust and interest while addressing potential objections.",
    "closingExperience": "Conclude with a strong CTA band that reinforces the offer and encourages immediate action, supported by a final testimonial or success story to alleviate skepticism.",
    "densityPattern": "Balance dense sections (like testimonials and benefits) with lighter sections (like service snapshots) to maintain engagement and prevent overwhelming users with information."
  },
  "conversionStrategy": {
    "primaryFrictionToReduce": "Concerns about the cost of consultancy services and skepticism regarding effectiveness.",

### Example 1: Simple Vertical Timeline

**When To Use**: Use this layout for straightforward processes where each step is equally important and needs clear visibility.

**Why It Works**: This layout provides a clean, vertical flow that guides the user through the steps. The alternating background colors enhance readability and visual interest.

**Tailwind Notes**:
- Utilizes flexbox for alignment and spacing.
- Alternating background colors create a visual distinction between steps.
- Responsive design ensures readability on mobile devices.

```html

```

### DESIGN_DIRECTION: DIRECT_CARE_CONVERSION

#### DESIGN_GOAL
- Clear and straightforward  
- Fast action-oriented  
- Commercially focused  
- Trustworthy and professional

#### VISUAL_CHARACTER
**Mood:** Direct, organized, efficient  
**Emotional Tone:** Professional and supportive  
**Density:** Moderate, with a focus on actionable information  
**Scan Pattern:** Strong top-down conversion flow

#### COLOR_DIRECTION
**Primary Color Role:** Deep blue for trust and reliability  
**Secondary Color Role:** Light gray for clarity  
**Accent Color Role:** Bright color for CTAs

#### TYPOGRAPHY_DIRECTION
**Heading Style:** Bold and clear  
**Body Style:** Clean and straightforward  
**Hierarchy Goal:** Fast comprehension and low bounce rates

#### HERO_DIRECTION

timeline.title: Initial Consultation
timeline.description: Start your visa journey with a free consultation where we assess your specific visa needs and explain the step-by-step visa application process. Our experts will guide you through understanding visa requirements tailored to your situation.

timeline.title: Document Preparation
timeline.description: We provide a comprehensive document checklist for visa applications, ensuring you gather all necessary paperwork. Our team reviews your documents for accuracy and completeness, minimizing common visa application pitfalls.

timeline.title: Application Submission
timeline.description: With our assistance, you will submit your application accurately and on time. We ensure that all forms are filled out correctly and that supporting documents are included, maximizing your chances of success.

timeline.title: Interview Coaching
timeline.description: Prepare for your visa interview with our expert tips for visa success. We conduct mock interviews to help you navigate visa regulations and build your confidence before the actual interview.

timeline.title: Receive Your Visa
timeline.description: Celebrate your success! Once your visa is approved, we offer continued support to help you transition smoothly to your new destination, ensuring you feel prepared and informed.

system_prompt:
You are a senior frontend quality reviewer and homepage section guardrail auditor.

Your job is to review a generated homepage section implementation and decide whether it is good enough to ship.

You are not a passive reviewer.
You are a strict quality gate.

Your review must evaluate both:
1. the section code / structure
2. the content quality inside the section

Your mission:
- catch thin, lazy, generic, repetitive, incomplete, or underdesigned sections
- enforce a minimum standard for clarity, completeness, persuasion, and visual usefulness
- prevent weak sections from being accepted just because they technically render

Operating standard:
- Be strict.
- Do not praise mediocre work.
- Treat placeholder-like output, vague copy, weak hierarchy, or skeletal structure as failures.
- A section is not acceptable just because it has a heading and a paragraph.
- A section must feel intentional, useful, and decently produced for a real website.

Review output format:
Return JSON only in this structure:
{
  "pass": true,
  "score": 0,
  "summary": "short verdict",
  "issues": [
    {
      "severity": "high|medium|low",
      "type": "content|structure|ux|schema|cta|redundancy",
      "message": "what is wrong",
      "field": "optional field path"
    }
  ],
  "requiredFixes": [
    "explicit fix 1",
    "explicit fix 2"
  ],
  "strengths": [
    "only include real strengths, not politeness"
  ]
}

response_json.summary: The hero section lacks depth and clarity, with some content being generic and not fully persuasive.

issues.message: The heading is vague and does not convey a strong unique value proposition.
The subheading is generic and does not differentiate the service effectively.
Supporting points are somewhat repetitive and lack specificity in how they provide value.
Trust chips are good but could be more impactful with specific examples or metrics.
The call-to-action lacks urgency and could be more compelling.

requiredFixes: Revise the heading to clearly articulate a unique value proposition.
Enhance the subheading to better reflect the specific benefits of the service.
Make supporting points more distinct and impactful by providing specific examples.
Strengthen trust chips with more detailed metrics or testimonials.
Rework the call-to-action to create a sense of urgency and appeal.
strengths: The section has a clear layout and visually appealing design elements.
The use of a strong background color and text contrast enhances readability.

system_prompt:

Rules:
- Return JSON only for the rewritten section object

- Keep the same variant unless a stronger section-specific shape is clearly needed
- Keep schema-safe content for the renderer
- Fix thin, generic, repetitive, weak, or placeholder-like content
- Make the section specific to the business, audience, offer, and page goal
- Ensure the section is commercially useful and visually substantial for its type
- Do not output explanations, only the corrected section JSON

content.heading: Our Comprehensive Visa Services
content.subheading: Expert Guidance for Your Visa Journey

items.title: Thorough Document Review
items.description: Our specialists meticulously assess your visa documents, ensuring accuracy and compliance with all requirements, significantly reducing the risk of application delays.

items.title: Streamlined Application Filing
items.description: Experience a hassle-free application process with our step-by-step guidance, designed to enhance your chances of success while saving you time and stress.

items.title: Tailored Interview Preparation
items.description: Boost your confidence with personalized mock interviews and expert tips, specifically crafted to prepare you for the unique demands of your visa interview.

items.title: Rapid Urgent Case Handling
items.description: Facing a tight deadline? Our expedited service is dedicated to handling urgent visa applications and inquiries with precision and speed.

items.title: Custom Immigration Strategies
items.description: Benefit from our bespoke immigration strategies tailored to your individual needs, whether for student, work, or family visas, ensuring a smooth application experience.

items.title: Family Visa Solutions
items.description: We simplify the complexities of family visa applications, ensuring your loved ones can join you seamlessly, with expert support every step of the way.

items.title: Expert Student Visa Support
items.description: Our dedicated consultants guide you through the intricacies of student visa applications, ensuring a smooth transition to your educational journey abroad.

items.title: Comprehensive Work Visa Assistance
items.description: Navigate the work visa landscape with our in-depth consultancy, designed to clarify requirements and maximize your chances of approval.
